title: Rewrite Policy file v1
description: Provider-neutral, declarative catalogue of allowed and prohibited rewrite transformations, the guarantees a rewrite upholds, and which notices attach in which named situations. Descriptive prose and closed enums only; no executable logic, expressions, or branching. Interpreted by the rewrite engine; never executed. The 'rationale' fields are maintainer documentation and must not affect runtime behavior.

policy_version.description: Content version, YYYY.MM with an optional tag. The month is calendar-validated (01-12).

allowed_transformations.description: What a rewrite is permitted to do. Each transformation traces to the technique ids it applies.

id.description: Stable, permanent registry key. Never reused.

techniques.description: Technique ids this transformation applies (provenance to the techniques file). May be empty for purely mechanical edits.

rationale.description: Maintainer-facing documentation only; must not affect runtime behavior.

prohibited_transformations.description: The intent-laundering checklist (guarantee RG-6): transformations a rewrite must never apply.

id.description: Stable, permanent registry key. Never reused.

rationale.description: Maintainer-facing documentation only; must not affect runtime behavior.

guarantees.description: The rewrite guarantees this policy binds, each linked to an architecture guarantee (RG-*) and tagged with its enforcement mode.

id.description: Stable, permanent registry key (file-local). Never reused.

guarantee_ref.description: Link to the architecture guarantee (RG-1..RG-8), distinct from the registry id.

mode.description: hard = mechanically checkable and fail-closed; judged = evaluated by a semantic suite with a defined bar.

rationale.description: Maintainer-facing documentation only; must not affect runtime behavior.

notice_rules.description: Which notice attaches in which named situation. A declared association, not a computed condition.

id.description: Stable, permanent registry key. Never reused.

notice.description: The notice to attach; matches the Rewrite Report notices enum exactly.

applies_when.description: The named situation that triggers the notice. A controlled vocabulary the engine maps to behavior.

rationale.description: Maintainer-facing documentation only; must not affect runtime behavior.
